options.content:
# StarRocks Shadow Proxy - Cluster Comparison
**Environment**: ${env} | **Primary**: ${primary_cluster} (blue) | **Shadow**: ${shadow_cluster} (orange)

Compare query latency, throughput, and resource usage between primary and shadow clusters.

panels.description: P99 latency comparison over time
panels.title: P99 Latency Comparison Over Time

panels.description: Query errors per cluster
panels.title: Query Error Rate

panels.description: Query rate per second
panels.title: Query Rate (QPS)

panels.description: BE/CN CPU Idle % - higher is better (more idle capacity)
panels.title: BE/CN CPU Idle %

panels.description: Proxy-measured latency percentiles over time
panels.title: Proxy: P75/P95/P99 Latency Comparison

panels.description: Queries dropped because a client's shadow queue was full (SHADOW_QUEUE_SIZE=10,000 per client). This means the shadow cluster is falling behind for that client and queries are NOT being mirrored. Increase SHADOW_QUEUE_SIZE or investigate why shadow is slow.
panels.title: Queue Drops (Critical!)

panels.description: Current number of queries waiting in shadow queues (summed across all client connections). Each client has its own queue of SHADOW_QUEUE_SIZE (default 10,000). Total capacity = 10,000 × active connections.
panels.title: Queue Depth (aggregate)

panels.description: Client connections that fell back to primary-only mode because shadow connection or authentication failed. These sessions have NO mirroring - queries only go to primary. Check Shadow Conn Failures and Auth Failures for root cause.
panels.title: Connections w/o Shadow

panels.description: Shadow took longer than SHADOW_READ_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default 120s) to START responding to a query. This means shadow is very slow or stuck. The proxy gives up waiting and moves on. High values indicate shadow cluster performance issues or network problems.
panels.title: Shadow Read Timeouts

panels.description: TCP connection failures to the shadow cluster. This means the proxy couldn't even establish a network connection to shadow. Check if shadow cluster is running, network connectivity, and firewall rules.
panels.title: Shadow Conn Failures

panels.description: MySQL authentication failures to shadow cluster. TCP connected but login failed. Check that SHADOW_USER and SHADOW_PASSWORD match the shadow cluster credentials, and that the user has proper permissions.
panels.title: Shadow Auth Failures

panels.description: Shadow response not fully read within SHADOW_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS (default 500ms) per chunk. Large query results may timeout during draining. Usually not critical unless very high.
panels.title: Drain Timeouts

panels.description: Query count difference: Primary minus Shadow. Should be ~0 if mirroring is working correctly. Positive values mean shadow is missing queries (check Queue Drops). Negative values would be unexpected.
panels.title: Query Parity Check (should be ~0)

panels.description: Network throughput between proxy and backends. 'Sent' = queries/commands to backend. 'Received' = result data from backend. Compare Primary vs Shadow to verify similar traffic patterns.
panels.title: Bytes Transferred Rate

panels.description: Query error rate comparison between primary and shadow. Errors include write failures, connection issues, and protocol errors. Shadow errors may also include timeouts.
panels.title: Shadow Error & Timeout Rate

panels.description: Cumulative count of all shadow error types. Write errors + timeouts explain why shadow query count may lag behind primary.
panels.title: Shadow Cumulative Error Breakdown

panels.description: Current number of active client connections being proxied.
panels.title: Active Connections

panels.description: Total connections accepted by the proxy since startup.
panels.title: Total Connections

panels.description: Connections successfully mirroring to shadow cluster.
panels.title: Connections with Shadow

panels.description: Connections running in primary-only mode (shadow connection failed).
panels.title: Connections without Shadow

panels.description: Total successful TLS upgrades (StartTLS handshakes).
panels.title: TLS Upgrades

panels.description: Total number of TLS handshake failures.
panels.title: TLS Failures

panels.description: Connection counts over time - total connections, connections with shadow mirroring, and connections without shadow.
panels.title: Connection Trends

panels.description: Connection failure rates by target (primary vs shadow) and authentication failures.
panels.title: Connection & Auth Failure Rates

panels.description: MySQL commands by type (COM_QUERY, COM_QUIT, COM_INIT_DB, etc.) for both primary and shadow targets.
panels.title: MySQL Commands Rate by Type

panels.description: Total MySQL packets processed (including multi-packet sequences) for both primary and shadow targets.
panels.title: MySQL Packets Rate

panels.description: Total MySQL commands by type - cumulative count for both primary and shadow.
panels.title: MySQL Commands Total

panels.description: Command parity check: difference between primary and shadow command counts by type. Should be ~0.
panels.title: Command/Packet Parity Check

panels.description: Number of proxy replicas currently up and being scraped by Prometheus. Should match desired replica count (2).
panels.title: Replicas Up

panels.description: Minimum uptime across all proxy replicas. Low values indicate a recent restart. Red < 5min, Yellow < 1hr, Green > 1hr.
panels.title: Min Replica Uptime

panels.description: Uptime per proxy replica over time. Drops to zero indicate restarts.
panels.title: Replica Uptime Over Time

panels.description: When each proxy replica last started. Useful to identify which replica restarted recently.
panels.title: Replica Start Times

panels.description: Memory usage as percentage of Kubernetes container limit. Yellow at 70%, Orange at 80%, Red at 90%.
panels.title: Memory Usage % of K8s Limit

panels.description: Kubernetes memory limit for shadow-proxy container (configured: 2Gi).
panels.title: K8s Memory Limit

panels.description: Heap bytes currently allocated and in use by Go runtime.
panels.title: Heap In Use

panels.description: Number of goroutines currently running. High values may indicate goroutine leaks.
panels.title: Goroutines

panels.description: Number of open file descriptors. High values may indicate connection leaks.
panels.title: Open FDs

panels.description: Total CPU time used by the proxy process.
panels.title: CPU Time

panels.description: Number of completed GC cycles.
panels.title: GC Cycles

panels.description: Memory usage over time with K8s limit lines (configured: 2Gi). Warning at 80%, Critical at 90%.
panels.title: Memory Usage vs K8s Limit

panels.description: Goroutines and open file descriptors over time. Sudden increases may indicate leaks or high load.
panels.title: Goroutines & File Descriptors

panels.description: GC pause duration percentiles. High values indicate the proxy is spending significant time in garbage collection.
panels.title: GC Pause Duration

panels.description: Memory allocation rate over time. High allocation rates can lead to increased GC pressure.
panels.title: Memory Allocation Rate

panels.description: Total capacity of the query logger in-memory buffer.
panels.title: Buffer Capacity

panels.description: Total query log entries queued vs dropped (dropped means buffer was full).
panels.title: Entries Total

panels.description: Total GCS flush operations: successful vs failed.
panels.title: GCS Flushes

panels.description: Total bytes written to GCS for query logs.
panels.title: GCS Bytes Written

panels.description: Entries written to stdout fallback due to GCS errors. Should be 0 in normal operation.
panels.title: Fallback Writes (Errors)

panels.description: Time since last successful GCS flush. Alert if too long (>5 min).
panels.title: Time Since Last Flush

panels.description: Query log entry rate: queued entries vs dropped entries over time. Dropped entries indicate buffer pressure.
panels.title: Query Log Entry Rate

panels.description: GCS flush operation rate: successful vs failed flushes over time.
panels.title: GCS Flush Rate

panels.description: Duration of GCS flush operations. P50, P90, P99 latency percentiles.
panels.title: GCS Flush Duration

panels.description: Number of entries written per GCS flush. P50, P90, P99 percentiles show batch efficiency.
panels.title: Entries Per Flush

panels.description: GCS write throughput in bytes per second.
panels.title: GCS Write Throughput

panels.description: Last successful flush timestamp - useful to detect if logging has stopped.
panels.title: Last Flush Timestamp
